label: has growth temperature optimum
definition: The optimal temperature at which an organism achieves maximum growth rate, measured in degrees Celsius.
example_usage: Unit: UO:0000027 (degree Celsius). Typical range: -5 to 120°C

label: has growth temperature minimum
definition: The minimum temperature at which an organism can grow, measured in degrees Celsius.
example_usage: Unit: UO:0000027. Represents lower boundary of growth temperature range.

label: has growth temperature maximum
definition: The maximum temperature at which an organism can grow, measured in degrees Celsius.
example_usage: Unit: UO:0000027. Represents upper boundary of growth temperature range.

label: has NaCl concentration optimum
definition: The optimal sodium chloride (NaCl) concentration for growth, expressed as weight/volume percentage.
example_usage: Unit: UO:0000187 (percent). Typical range: 0-30%. See also CHEBI:26710 (sodium chloride)

label: has NaCl concentration minimum
definition: The minimum sodium chloride (NaCl) concentration tolerated for growth, expressed as weight/volume percentage.
example_usage: Unit: UO:0000187. Represents lower boundary of salinity tolerance range.

label: has NaCl concentration maximum
definition: The maximum sodium chloride (NaCl) concentration tolerated for growth, expressed as weight/volume percentage.
example_usage: Unit: UO:0000187. Represents upper boundary of salinity tolerance range.

label: has pH optimum
definition: The optimal pH value for growth on the pH scale (0-14).
example_usage: No unit needed (pH scale). Typical range: 0-14. See also PATO:0001842 (acidity)

label: has pH minimum
definition: The minimum pH value at which growth can occur.
example_usage: Represents lower boundary of pH tolerance range.

label: has pH maximum
definition: The maximum pH value at which growth can occur.
example_usage: Represents upper boundary of pH tolerance range.

label: assimilates
definition: A relation between an organism and a chemical entity, where the organism takes up and incorporates the chemical into its biomass or metabolic intermediates.
notes: Broader than 'uses as carbon source' (METPO:2000006). Includes uptake of any nutrient for anabolic/catabolic purposes. See also GO:0006091

label: uses as energy source
definition: A relation between an organism and a chemical entity, where the organism oxidizes or otherwise catabolizes the chemical primarily for energy (ATP) generation rather than for carbon or other biosynthetic building blocks.
notes: Distinct from carbon source (METPO:2000006 - biomass) and electron donor/acceptor (redox). Focused on ATP/energy metabolism. See also GO:0006091

label: uses as nitrogen source
definition: A relation between an organism and a nitrogen-containing chemical entity, where the organism assimilates the nitrogen for biosynthesis of amino acids, nucleotides, and other nitrogenous compounds.
notes: May already exist as METPO:2000014 - needs verification. If 2000014 exists, use that ID instead. Includes inorganic (ammonia, nitrate) and organic nitrogen (amino acids). See also GO:0006807

label: uses as electron donor
definition: A relation between an organism and a chemical entity, where the organism oxidizes the chemical to obtain electrons for energy metabolism, respiration, or photosynthesis.
notes: Complements METPO:2000008 (uses as electron acceptor). Together they describe complete electron transport chains. See also GO:0022900

label: produces acid from
definition: A relation between an organism and a chemical substrate, where the organism produces acid from the substrate via fermentation or metabolism.
notes: More specific than METPO:2000202 (produces). Indicates acidification as metabolic outcome. Important for fermentation characterization and diagnostic tests. See also GO:0019660

label: produces gas from
definition: A relation between an organism and a chemical substrate, where the organism produces gas (CO2, H2, CH4, etc.) from the substrate via metabolism.
notes: More specific than METPO:2000202 (produces). Gas production includes CO2 (respiration/fermentation), H2 (fermentation), CH4 (methanogenesis), N2 (denitrification).

label: produces base from
definition: A relation between an organism and a chemical substrate, where the organism produces base or alkaline products from the substrate via metabolism.
notes: More specific than METPO:2000202 (produces). Indicates alkalinization as metabolic outcome. Less common than acid production in fermentation.
